kept.text:
$TSM: TSMC locks in a 30-year power purchase agreement for 294MW from Hai Long offshore wind farm in Taiwan.

This move secures long-term renewable energy for one of the world's biggest chip manufacturers. With this deal, TSMC is aiming to power its operations sustainably while tackling rising energy costs. That’s a solid play as they ramp up production amid global chip demand.

In my opinion, this isn’t just about going green; it’s about securing their future in a competitive market. If they can pull this off, they might just set the standard for others in the industry. 🌬️⚡️

tweet.text:
$TSM Here is what the market does not understand.

Apple is in exploratory talks with Intel and Samsung for SECONDARY foundry capacity.

This is NOT a preference. TSMC capacity are fully consumed by advanced nodes, by AI accelerator demand from $NVDA $AMD $GOOGL. TSMC is MAXED OUT.

Intel and Samsung are not equals. They are compromises. Power, yield, and reliability do not survive the comparison.

You would only know this if you have lived inside a fab, a process or device engineer.

quoted_tweet.text:
What is Artificial Intelligence?

AI is more than a buzzword. It's a fundamental shift in how technology works.

Artificial Intelligence refers to the development and application of systems and software programs that simulate and enhance human cognitive functions — enabling machines to perform tasks traditionally requiring human intelligence, including decision-making, visual perception, speech recognition, language translation and adaptive learning from data.

Dan IVES Wedbush AI Revolution ETF (Ticker: IVES) tracks companies at the heart of this shift.

Learn more at https://t.co/ulBi6nSIEp

tweet.text:
THE CPU SHORTAGE IS HERE AND KICKING $AMD $INTC $NVDA $TSM

At our Financial Analyst Day in November, we outlined a server CPU market growing at approximately 18% annually over the next 3 to 5 years.

Based on the demand signals we are seeing today and the structural increase in CPU compute requirements driven by Agentic AI, we now expect the server CPU TAM to grow at greater than 35% annually, reaching over $120 billion by 2030. 

Response to this demand, we are working closely with our supply chain partners to meaningfully increase our wafer and back end capacities to support this growth. As a result, we now expect server CPU revenue to grow by more than 70% year over year in the second quarter, with robust growth continuing through the second half of 2026 and into 2027.

tweet.text:
The 2024-2025 Skeptics vs. 2026 Reality

I still remember my ex-patreon subscribers in both 2024 and 2025 who questioned why I kept charting $NVDA, $AMD, $TSM, $AVGO, $MU and $SOXL 5 times a week. “Can these stocks really keep going up?” they asked.

I chart them for a reason — they are the clear stars of the AI era. I don’t waste even one minute of my life or yours highlighting laggards or stocks in long-term downtrends… until proven otherwise.

Fast forward to 2026: every single one is up over 3x, with SOXL delivering more than 13x. I’ve shared this thesis for free on X for years, constantly reminding people that semiconductors should form the majority of any growth portfolio. Most ignored it — everyone has their own convictions and strategies.

I can only share what I see. Sadly, I can’t give personal financial advice!
